title: [Feature]: Evaluate Hadoop ecosystem sinks
problem: Oracle GoldenGate Big Data targets include Apache HDFS, HBase, Hive, Flume, Cloudera Hadoop, Cloudera Data Platform, Hortonworks Data Platform, and MapR. nats-sinks does not yet define whether these older and enterprise Hadoop targets should become individual sinks or compatibility profiles.
proposal: Research Hadoop ecosystem sink support as a family. The evaluation should split HDFS file/object-style writes, HBase row-key writes, Hive table ingestion, Flume event forwarding, and distribution-specific compatibility. It should decide which targets still have enough user demand and maintained Python integration options to justify implementation.
users: Organizations with existing Hadoop estates that need controlled event persistence from NATS JetStream.
delivery_semantics: Each selected target must define its own durable commit boundary. HDFS rename-based commits, HBase row writes, Hive table commits, and Flume forwarding have different ACK implications and must not be treated as interchangeable.
security: Kerberos, TLS, service principals, HDFS paths, table names, row keys, and payloads are sensitive. Path traversal and unsafe filename generation must be prevented.
acceptance: Research separates HDFS, HBase, Hive, Flume, and distribution-specific support.
Deprecated or unsupported ecosystems are explicitly marked not planned if appropriate.
Any accepted connector has a separate implementation issue.
Unit tests are possible without live Hadoop services.
Documentation warns about maintenance and operational complexity.
tests: Research-only initially. Future connectors need fake clients, path-safety tests, duplicate handling tests, and gated integration tests.
documentation: Add Hadoop ecosystem evaluation notes to sink roadmap documentation.
closeout: Close after the evaluation release is published and follow-up feature requests are created.
